_comment: Phase-1 artifact deck — the published PRD for the trace-column pet, shown full-screen in kitsoki via deck-review. Authored as the validated PM output.

scenes.title: A little pet for the trace column
scenes.subtitle: Give the long agent-loop wait some life — without taking screen space
scenes.narration: The product manager's validated output: a PRD for a small companion that lives in the trace column.

scenes.lede: Watching a long run is a lonely wait.
scenes.body: The trace column just ticks rows by while the agent thinks, tests run, and a fix lands. Nothing on the screen is alive. A small ambient companion can add warmth to that wait — and cost nothing.
scenes.narration: Watching a long session is a lonely wait at the trace column. Nothing on screen is alive. A small ambient companion adds warmth to that wait, and costs nothing.

scenes.title: What it is
scenes.narration: One small animated S-V-G pet, anchored at the bottom of the trace column. It idles, blinks, wanders, and now and then a snack appears and it wanders over to eat it.

cards.label: Lives at the column bottom
cards.sub: anchored to .iv__trace, never displaces a row

cards.label: Idles & plays
cards.sub: bobs, blinks, wanders, the occasional snack & poop

cards.label: Opt-in
cards.sub: off by default; one toggle turns it on

cards.label: Zero weight
cards.sub: inline SVG + CSS keyframes — no images, no deps, no network

scenes.title: Goals & guardrails
scenes.narration: The goals are simple and the guardrails are strict: opt-in, ambient, and weightless.

cells: A small animated SVG pet at the trace-column bottom
Absolutely positioned — never blocks or displaces trace rows
Pure inline SVG + CSS — no images, deps, or network

scenes.body: A published PRD and an HTML mockup of the pet in the real drive layout — the validated product output that the architect picks up next.
scenes.narration: The validated output: a published PRD and an HTML mockup of the pet in the real drive layout, ready for the architect to pick up.
